item.title: Editorial: Risankizumab in Refractory Crohn's Disease-From Real-World Effectiveness to Strategic Positioning and Early Monitoring.

item.description: Intestinal research - This final long-term extension (LTE) analysis assessed the long-term efficacy and safety of ustekinumab (UST) in the Asian patient subpopulation of the UNITI/IM-UNITI study. Patients completing saf...

item.title: Efficacy and safety of ozanimod in Japanese patients with moderately to severely active ulcerative colitis naive to immunomodulators or advanced therapies: post hoc analysis of the phase 2/3 J-True North study.
item.description: Intestinal research - There is limited evidence for ozanimod, an oral sphingosine 1-phosphate receptor modulator, in patients with ulcerative colitis (UC) before treatment is escalated to an immunomodulator (IM) or adva...

item.title: Efficacy of Ustekinumab on Skin Reactions Induced by Tumor Necrosis Factor-Alpha Inhibitors in Patients with Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Inflammatory intestinal diseases - Tumor necrosis factor-alpha (TNF-α) inhibitors sometimes induce skin reactions in patients with Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC). Ustekinumab (UST) has been reported to be effective...

item.title: Real-World Effectiveness, Safety and Quality of Life With Ustekinumab for the Treatment of Crohn's Disease: Results of the UK Ustekinumab Real World Evidence Study (UndieS) Study Group.
item.description: Alimentary pharmacology & therapeutics - While randomised controlled trials have demonstrated the efficacy of ustekinumab for treating Crohn's disease (CD), real-world effectiveness and patient experience in heterogeneous clinical populat...

item.title: Clinical characteristics, treatment and prognosis of ixekizumab-induced inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Immunopharmacology and immunotoxicology - Background： To explore the clinical characteristics of ixekizumab-induced inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and provide references for prevention, diagnosis and treatment.Methods： Clinical reports o...

item.title: Phage intervention improves colitis and response to corticosteroids by attenuating virulence of Crohn's disease-associated bacteria.
item.description: Science translational medicine - Adherent-invasive Escherichia coli (AIEC) exhibits proinflammatory properties and has been implicated in the pathogenesis of Crohn's disease (CD), a form of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Antibi...

item.title: Early real-world effectiveness of mirikizumab in a difficult-to-treat ulcerative colitis cohort.

item.description: Gastroenterologia y hepatologia - Elderly patients are an increasingly relevant group in inflammatory bowel disease, yet clinical trials often exclude them. Ustekinumab might be a suitable treatment due to its adequate clinical and...

item.title: Cancer risk with methotrexate-TNF antagonist combination therapy compared to TNF-antagonist monotherapy in IBD.

item.title: Risk of Infections With Infliximab vs Adalimumab Among Children With Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: JAMA network open - Infliximab and adalimumab are the only biologics approved by the US Food and Drug Administration for use in children with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). The comparative infection risks between i...

item.title: Correction to: P0562 Comparative Efficacy and Safety of Upadacitinib Versus Oral Prednisolone for the Induction of Remission in Ulcerative Colitis: A Prospective, Multicenter, Open-label, Randomized Controlled Trial (PREDUPA Trial) Protocol and P0563 Mirikizumab Demonstrated Sustained and Durable Long-Term Efficacy and Favorable Safety in Week 52 Endoscopic Responders with Crohn's disease: 3-year VIVID-2 Open-Label Extension Interim Results.

item.title: pH-responsive microgels containing multifunctional ginsenoside Rh2-based liposomes remodel intestinal environment to enhance celastrol therapy for ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Materials today. Bio - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease closely associated with intestinal mucosal barrier damage, abnormal immune responses, and gut microbiota dysbiosis. Celastrol (Cel), ...

item.title: Synergistic effects of partial enteral nutrition combined with Ustekinumab in induction and maintenance therapy for Crohn's disease: Real-world experience from a multicentre cohort.
item.description: Clinical nutrition (Edinburgh, Scotland) - Ustekinumab (UST) is effective for Crohn's disease (CD) but has some limitations. Partial enteral nutrition (PEN) may be helpful for CD remission, yet its ability to augment UST efficacy remains un...

item.title: Intestinal subepithelial myofibroblasts in inflammatory bowel disease: fibroblast heterogeneity, fibrosis, and therapeutic targeting.
item.description: Frontiers in medicine - Review - Intestinal fibrosis remains one of the most challenging complications of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), frequently leading to bowel strictures, impaired intestinal function, and surgical interve...
